key_points: Deering library praised as beautiful study spot with good vibes
Hinman dorm recently renovated and reopened to student curiosity
No major housing horror stories but campus distances are a complaint
key_quotes: Got a lot of work done in here. Good spot and weirdly, good memories?
wish it was less busy cuz i love the vibes
I need someone to tell me everything about the new 1835 Hinman

key_points: Making friends freshman year is genuinely hard; students warn of cutthroat culture
Bullying of incoming freshmen on class Instagram pages reported by students
LGBTQ org homo_nu outed hundreds of students via speed-dating data leak
key_quotes: making friends at NU is quite tough, especially during the first year when you're getting adjusted to a lot of stuff. People at NU can be quite cutthroat
homo_nu condoned violence, hate, and harassment toward members of the queer community on campus
My name appeared on the homo_nu data leak. Because of political reasons, I was working hard to protect my identity

key_points: MOD pizza closed; replacement is similar but noticeably more expensive
New salads lack included protein unless you pay extra
Limited specific dining feedback; food quality not a major Reddit discussion
key_quotes: MOD's salad used to be my favorite protein source... the new pizza/salad place so similar to MOD but more costly
The new salads don't come with any protein unless you pay more
Go to frio gelato! You can't be sad if you're eating ice cream

key_points: Quarter system pace causes severe burnout; students report feeling constantly inadequate
Staff losing affordable therapy access after insurance switch to UnitedHealthcare
Winter seasonal depression is a documented, serious concern for students from warmer climates
key_quotes: I sleep and I feel like I wasted time. I eat and I feel like I wasted time. I'm seriously crashing out
I will likely stop therapy because God knows Northwestern isn't paying me enough to spend $40 per week on therapy
You never feel how bad the weather makes you until the first glimpses of spring come out and you realize it was really seasonal depression

key_points: Aid packages can be cut dramatically mid-enrollment with little warning
Appeals work — students who pushed back received aid restored
FGLI students report financial anxiety as a constant undercurrent at NU
key_quotes: my aid has been cut by 40%??? costs increasing to 92k is insane
Northwestern returned ALL of the scholarship money they had given me last year and then some. PLEASE, PLEASE, PLEASE, APPEAL YOUR FINANCIAL AID
This is very dependent on your financial status. If you're very high income or very low income, Northwestern is a great deal. The middle is brutal

key_points: Quarter system is brutally fast; students routinely underperform expectations first year
No grade inflation unlike peer schools — grades here actually mean something
Pre-med track is competitive but research opportunities are genuinely strong
key_quotes: I worked decently hard on each of my classes, put in the late nights and days with little sleep... all I have to show for it are below average grades
we don't have grade inflation like many other similar schools
Many, many premeds do great research, have a fantastic time, and go on to good med schools. Don't let redditors disillusion you with anecdotes

key_points: President Schill resigned amid widespread staff and faculty anger; legacy described as 'god-awful'
Over 425 staff laid off in 2025 while executive salaries and stadium spending continued
Administration capitulated to Trump funding threats, reversing prior agreements with student body
key_quotes: What a god-awful legacy he'll leave
Northwestern exist to grow the endowment now, that is the goal
Pay a blackmailer once, pay them for life

key_points: Campus on Lake Michigan is genuinely stunning; lake walks are a student favorite
Evanston winter is brutal — nose-hair-freezing cold with face-cutting lake wind
Chicago access is real but requires effort; not a walkable urban campus
key_quotes: the wind coming from the lake literally cuts your face. You can routinely see people wearing full face masks
I love walking along the lake from campus. So relaxing, so pretty
January and February are cold in a special way, the lake effect is real

key_points: NU name carries weight in Midwest and finance but less so in tech vs. MIT/Stanford/CMU
Job market brutal for all new grads; 200 applications for one offer is common
Medill journalism and Kellogg business connections are genuinely elite pipelines
key_quotes: Northwestern's name doesn't carry the same weight in tech as the juggernauts like MIT/Stanford/Carnegie Mellon, but it certainly carries more weight than your average state school
I applied to ~250 internships my junior year and only heard back from around 5 companies
You are underestimating how brutal the current job market is right now. Getting 1 offer with 200 applications is considered good

key_points: Total cost hitting $92k/year; middle-income families hit hardest by aid formula
Staff describe below-market wages with purchasing power down 11% since 2019
$862M new football stadium being built while staff benefits slashed and raises frozen
key_quotes: No merit/cost of living increases this year; no back filling of staff positions; reduced tuition benefits; switch from BCBS to United HC. Go Cats!!!!
Despite what the administration says, a 1 BILLION dollar football stadium is a drain on financial resources. This is where our merit increases went
Staff work for below-market-rate wages because it's balanced out with benefits. Instead we're hit with austerity measures

key_points: Academically intense with a 'work hard but feel behind' culture dominating student experience
Political tension high; pro-Palestine protests, mandatory antisemitism training, and Qatar contract all flashpoints
Strong alumni pride and school spirit coexist with genuine institutional frustration
key_quotes: I feel like no matter how hard I try, I can't relate to anyone here
Northwestern is on the wrong side of history on this one. It's gross to see and my remaining purple pride has disintegrated
This is always in the right. 'Go Cats' when spotting NU ppl off campus is a ritual

key_points: **Administration:** 425+ layoffs, frozen raises, and gutted benefits while building $862M stadium
**Political/Institutional:** University signed Qatar contract forbidding criticism of regime; capitulated to federal pressure reversing student agreements
**Student Wellbeing:** LGBTQ org outed hundreds of students; homo_nu deleted complaints and blocked critics instead of apologizing
key_quotes: Thank god the staff lost their tuition benefits halfway through their degree programs
Northwestern's Contract With Qatar Forbids School From Criticizing Regime
homo_nu has committed similar actions, if not worse, countless times. Personal information about others relationships, sexual preferences, etc, have all been posted publicly

key_points: Lake Michigan shoreline walks are a free, stunning stress-reliever students swear by
Deering Library interior is architecturally beautiful and underutilized for studying
Chicago is a real cultural resource — PhD students especially can live there full-time
key_quotes: I try to do a walk around campus at least once a week in the summer. So beautiful!
Got a lot of work done in here. Good spot and weirdly, good memories?
Wait till you see the closed stacks where they keep the books
